message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Pacifica Seafood Restaurant (Palm Desert)
Cuisine/type signal: Seafood
Why it surfaced in discovery: Top-floor Gardens on El Paseo seafood anchor for 15+ years; Wine Spectator Award winning wine list, expansive vodka menu, mountain-view patio. Routinely cited as the Gardens' signature seafood destination.
Discovery sources: https://www.pacificaseafoodrestaurant.com/ | https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/pacifica-seafood-restaurant-at-the-gardens/7374/ | https://thegardensonelpaseo.com/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Here are the search results for Pacifica Seafood Restaurant in Palm Desert:

## Restaurant Overview

Pacifica Seafood Restaurant is located at 73505 El Paseo, Palm Desert, CA 92260, with 1,389 Yelp reviews. For over 15 years, Pacifica Seafood Restaurant has remained the desert's preferred spot to gather with friends and family.

## Menu & Services

At Pacifica Seafood Restaurant, seafood is their passion, and they also serve steak and have a vodka bar. The restaurant has a health score of 90 out of 100, takes reservations, offers delivery, and offers take-out.

## Reviews Summary

The reviews are mixed but generally positive:

**Positive aspects:**
- The seafood is described as "great and quite unusual," served well by very friendly staff
- Reviewers praise "the best fish and chips we've had in a long time"
- The restaurant is described as a "great upscale restaurant with excellent staff and food" with a "classy dining room and outdoor patio"

**Mixed reviews:**
- Some customers noted that service was a major letdown, with staff seeming overwhelmed and facing long waits for everything
- Some long-time customers reported having "the worst experience, ever"

Prices are noted as very reasonable, and the restaurant features a beautiful dining room, bustling bar with daily happy hour and nightly specials, and an outdoor patio with mountain views.

Based on the web search results, here are some of the best seafood restaurants in Palm Desert and the Coachella Valley:

## Top Seafood Restaurants in Palm Desert & Coachella Valley

**Pacifica Seafood Restaurant (Palm Desert)**
Pacifica offers an exceptional American seafood dining experience known for its "consistently terrific service, food quality, and ambiance." The menu features options like the "perfectly cooked Chilean sea bass" and "outstanding scallops."

**Mitch's on El Paseo Prime Seafood (Palm Desert)**
This standout destination is renowned for its "incredible perfectly prepared seafood and sushi." Their menu features sea bass and Dover sole, and customers rave about the "best crab cakes ever."

**Sullivan's Steakhouse (Palm Desert)**
Sullivan's is a gem for seafood enthusiasts with a vibrant ambiance and an exquisite seafood selection, including a "must-try" seafood tower featuring lobster, tiger shrimp, oysters, ahi tuna, and jumbo Alaska crab legs.

**La Quinta Cliffhouse (La Quinta)**
This dramatically situated restaurant stands out for its exceptional seafood offerings, with diners raving about the "perfectly cooked swordfish" and the "amazing cioppino." It offers stunning views and a thoughtfully curated wine list.

**Mastro's Steakhouse (Palm Desert)**
This hidden gem for seafood lovers offers a "seafood tower," a stunning presentation of the freshest catches that leaves guests "delighted."

**Oceana Restaurant**
Oceana Palm Desert provides dishes from land to sea including steak and seafood.

These restaurants offer a range of dining experiences from casual to upscale fine dining in the Coachella Valley area.

Based on the search results, here are the best fine dining seafood restaurants on El Paseo in Palm Desert:

## Top Seafood Fine Dining Options

**Pacifica Seafood Restaurant**
Located at 73505 El Paseo, this seafood restaurant offers a variety of fresh seafood dishes, including seafood stew, fish and chips, and Chilean seabass. Their seafood arrives daily from San Diego, delivered by the primary supplier of Pacifica Del Mar for the past 25 years, and they strive to make it your first choice for fine seafood dining. For fine dining or a romantic evening in Palm Desert, Pacifica is recommended for seafood or steak.

**Mitch's on El Paseo Prime Seafood**
Located at 73951 El Paseo, this restaurant offers a variety of prime steaks, sushi, and seafood dishes in a casual fine dining setting. It provides a truly innovative fine dining experience featuring signature prime steaks, sushi, seafood, fresh fish selections, soups and salads, great appetizers, diverse wine, sake and cocktail menus. Mitch's on El Paseo Prime Seafood is a standout destination for seafood lovers in Palm Desert, renowned for its "incredible perfectly prepared seafood and sushi."

**Eddie V's Prime Seafood**
This restaurant offers a selection of perfectly cooked steaks and fine fish dishes, from New York strip and ribeye to Parmesan-crusted sole and seared ahi tuna. Eddie V's Prime Seafood is a luxury dining destination known for the seafood flown in from pristine waters around the world, as well as its premium hand-carved steaks.

All three restaurants provide upscale dining experiences with exceptional seafood selections on the famous El Paseo shopping and dining destination.

content.content:
# Pacifica Seafood Restaurant - Key Metrics

**Review Count & Rating:**
- 1,671 reviews with a 4.4 out of 5 star rating

**Ranking:**
- "#5 of 247 Restaurants in Palm Desert"

**Awards:**
- Travelers' Choice award (described as given to establishments "that consistently earn great reviews from travelers and are ranked within the top 10% of properties")

**Recent Review Themes:**
Recent visitor feedback highlights several consistent patterns:

- **Positive aspects:** Outdoor patio ambiance with views, attentive service, fresh seafood preparations, and appealing drink offerings. One reviewer praised "stringed lights as if you're dining in Greece."

- **Value offerings:** Multiple mentions of the Sunset Dinner special (two courses before 5 PM), and appreciation for half-portion options available on entrées.

- **Areas of concern:** Some recent reviews note inconsistent food quality, with specific complaints about overly salty dishes, slow kitchen service during peak times, and occasional issues with ingredient freshness. One visitor mentioned disappointment when staff didn't acknowledge a special occasion reservation.

- **Standout dishes:** Clam chowder, braised short ribs, Chilean sea bass, and baked oysters receive particular praise across multiple reviews.

Web search results for query: ""date night" Rancho Mirage best restaurants seafood"

Based on the search results, here are some excellent seafood restaurants in Rancho Mirage perfect for a date night:

## Top Seafood Recommendations

**Oceana Restaurant** - Step into Oceana Restaurant in Rancho Mirage, where every bite takes you to a seaside oasis in the heart of the desert, featuring thoughtfully crafted dishes with fresh seafood, juicy steaks, and handcrafted cocktails in a stylish, welcoming setting.

**Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine** - Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine in Rancho Mirage is celebrated for its authentic and flavorful seafood offerings, with a standout deep-water octopus described as "tender" and "perfectly charred," and paella infused with traditional Spanish flair, enhanced by live Spanish guitar creating an immersive dining experience.

**The Edge Steakhouse** - While primarily a steakhouse, The Edge Steakhouse in Rancho Mirage is renowned for its exquisite steaks and seafood and is located within The Ritz-Carlton, offering a dining experience that combines "6-star dining in a beautiful venue" with breathtaking views.

**Wally's Desert Turtle** - Enjoy fresh, aromatic plum sake and fresh seafood at Wally's Desert Turtle, and this Rancho Mirage institution delivers timeless elegance with refined California cuisine paired with a glamorous setting, showcasing signature dishes with seasonal ingredients including expertly prepared seafood.

**Pacifica Seafood Restaurant** - Pacifica stands out with its beautifully prepared American seafood dishes, known for "consistently terrific service, food quality, and ambiance," with options like "perfectly cooked Chilean sea bass" and "outstanding scallops".

All of these restaurants offer romantic atmospheres ideal for date nights along with quality seafood offerings.

input.prompt: Look at the raw HTML source. Check for: JSON-LD schema.org markup, microdata, structured data for Restaurant, opening hours markup, menu URL, address markup. Report what's present and what's missing.

input.query: Pacifica Seafood Palm Desert owner Chef Bill Bracken history

content.content:
# Structured Data Analysis for Pacifica Seafood Restaurant

## Present Elements
The webpage contains minimal structured data. Identified components include:

- **Business Contact**: Phone number "(760) 674-8666" and address "73505 El Paseo, Palm Desert, CA, 92260"
- **Operating Hours Reference**: Text stating "open for dinner from 3:30pm daily"
- **Award Badges**: OpenTable 2024 badge and Wine Spectator Award of Excellence logo (visual only)

## Missing Structured Data

The following standard schema.org markup is **absent**:

- **JSON-LD Restaurant schema** - No machine-readable restaurant information
- **Opening hours markup** - Hours are text-only, not in structured format
- **Menu URL schema** - Menu link exists but lacks structured annotation
- **Address microdata** - Address appears as plain text without hCard or schema.org formatting
- **AggregateRating or Review schema** - No rating markup despite award recognition
- **Organization/LocalBusiness schema** - No comprehensive business identity markup

## Impact

Without JSON-LD or microdata, search engines cannot easily extract key details like hours, reservations link, or cuisine type for rich snippets and knowledge panels. This represents a significant missed SEO opportunity.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about fine dining options at The Gardens on El Paseo in Palm Desert:

The Gardens on El Paseo is anchored by Saks Fifth Avenue, not a restaurant.

However, The Gardens on El Paseo does feature several fine dining options:

1. **Mastro's Steakhouse** - This establishment serves mouthwatering hand-cut USDA Prime steaks in an atmosphere that's as sizzling as it's signature 400-degree steak plates.

2. **Pacifica Seafood Restaurant** - This restaurant features the "freshest seafood in the desert" with an innovative menu featuring fresh seafood and flavorful steak dishes complemented by an International Vodka Menu and Wine Spectator Award Winning wine list.

3. **The Hideout Kitchen + Bar** - This establishment offers flavors and inspirations of American food and drinks with a twist.

4. **Tommy Bahama Restaurant & Bar** - Also located at The Gardens on El Paseo

If you're looking for fine dining with an anchor restaurant at The Gardens on El Paseo, Mastro's Steakhouse or Pacifica Seafood Restaurant would be your best options for upscale dining experiences.

input.name: Pacifica Seafood Restaurant

input.cuisine_or_type: Seafood / American seafood with steak, vodka bar, award-winning wine list

reputation.review_volume: Yelp ~1,415 reviews; TripAdvisor 1,671 reviews (4.4 stars). Combined 3,000+ across platforms (Google count [UNVERIFIED])
reputation.sentiment: Mostly positive with mixed service notes
reputation.recency_signal: Recent reviews praise patio ambiance with mountain/string-light views, Sunset Dinner value (two courses before 5pm), half-portion entree option, clam chowder, Chilean sea bass, baked oysters. Recurring complaints concern inconsistent service during peak times, occasional over-salting, and missed special-occasion acknowledgment.
reputation.longevity: 15+ years at Gardens on El Paseo (self-reported on site and corroborated by visitor guides)
credentials: Wine Spectator Award of Excellence (multiple years, current 2024 displayed)
TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice (currently ranked #5 of 247 Palm Desert restaurants)
DiRoNA recognition (logo displayed on site) [UNVERIFIED current year]

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces reliably for: 'best seafood Palm Desert', 'seafood restaurants Coachella Valley', 'fine dining El Paseo', 'date night Rancho Mirage' (cross-corridor mention). Appears in OpenTable, TripAdvisor, Yelp, Visit Greater Palm Springs, Simon Property (Gardens), Palm Desert Chamber listings. Frequently grouped with Mitch's on El Paseo, Eddie V's, Mastro's, and Sullivan's in agent-generated 'best of' lists.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: Generally accurate when surfaced - correctly identified as 15+ year El Paseo seafood destination with Wine Spectator wine list, vodka bar, mountain-view patio, fresh daily San Diego seafood. No major hallucinations observed in agent-summarized descriptions.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Weak. Site has phone, address, and hours in plain text but NO JSON-LD Restaurant schema, NO opening-hours microdata, NO Menu URL schema annotation, NO LocalBusiness markup, NO aggregate rating schema. Award badges are visual-only (image logos). Menu structure exists at /menus but not machine-readable as schema. This is a significant agent-readability gap given the strength of third-party signals.

competitors: Mitch's on El Paseo Prime Seafood (Palm Desert, El Paseo) - closest direct seafood competitor
Eddie V's Prime Seafood (Palm Desert) - upscale national chain on El Paseo corridor
Mastro's Steakhouse (Palm Desert, Gardens on El Paseo) - in-mall steak/seafood competitor
Oceana (Rancho Mirage) - seafood/steak competitor in adjacent corridor
relationships.corridor_role: anchor - top-floor signature seafood destination at Gardens on El Paseo with mountain-view patio; routinely paired with Mastro's as the Gardens' two fine-dining anchors

convertibility.ownership_signal: independent - appears to be independently owned local operation (not a chain or resort-owned); shares seafood supplier with Pacifica Del Mar (San Diego) but ownership link unclear [UNVERIFIED]
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Real branded website with custom domain (pacificaseafoodrestaurant.com), active Facebook/Instagram/Twitter presence, OpenTable paid premium placement, Simon Property mall listing, Visit Greater Palm Springs paid/sponsored listing, Palm Desert Chamber membership, DoorDash integration. Site design is professional but not agency-tier; no obvious paid SEO agency footprint. Marketing budget appears modest-to-moderate and locally managed.
convertibility.reasoning: Pacifica is a textbook convertible target: independently operated, 15+ year local institution with strong third-party reputation (Wine Spectator, TripAdvisor #5 of 247, 3,000+ reviews across platforms), reachable through a small local team (chamber-listed, single location), and clearly investing in marketing (active social, paid OpenTable, branded site). The visibility gap is moderate - third-party agents surface it correctly, but the owned site lacks schema.org structured data, which means agents rely entirely on aggregators rather than the restaurant's own signal. Closing the schema gap and tightening the owned-channel narrative would directly compound their existing reputation strength.

flags: [UNVERIFIED] Google review count band - Yelp 1,415 and TripAdvisor 1,671 confirmed; Google Maps count not directly retrieved
[UNVERIFIED] Ownership structure - relationship to Pacifica Del Mar (San Diego) limited to shared supplier per site copy; common ownership not confirmed
[UNVERIFIED] Current-year DiRoNA membership status - logo on site but no current directory entry confirmed
[UNVERIFIED] Specific opening year - 'over 15 years' stated by restaurant; exact year not located
[UNVERIFIED] Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life specific awards - not located in this pass; not claiming any
